analysis.summary: The technician connected to a server, identified six critical services that were stopped in Server Manager, and proceeded to manually start each of these services. After confirming all services were running and the server status was healthy, the technician locked the remote session.
actions_taken: Observed red indicators for 'Local Server' and 'All Servers' showing 6 services were not running.
Clicked on the 'Services' link under 'All Servers' to view details.

Identified and reviewed the list of 6 stopped services: HuntressUpdater, Clipboard User Service_a05de, Diagnostic Policy Service, Huntress Agent Service, Update Orchestrator Service, and User Access Logging Service on server TE-CL-TECHDC-01.
Right-clicked and selected 'Start Services' for HuntressUpdater.
Right-clicked and selected 'Start Services' for Clipboard User Service_a05de.
Right-clicked and selected 'Start Services' for Diagnostic Policy Service (DPS).
Right-clicked and selected 'Start Services' for Huntress Agent Service (HuntressAgent).
Right-clicked and selected 'Start Services' for Update Orchestrator Service (UsoSvc).
Right-clicked and selected 'Start Services' for User Access Logging Service (UALSvc).
Confirmed all 6 services were now running.
Clicked 'OK' to close the Services Detail View window.
Verified the Server Manager Dashboard displayed healthy status (green) for all roles and services.
issues_observed: Server Manager dashboard initially showed red warning status for 'Local Server' and 'All Servers' due to 6 stopped services.
issues_resolved: The 6 stopped services were successfully started, resolving the critical alerts in Server Manager and restoring the server to a healthy status.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician efficiently navigated to the problem area in Server Manager and systematically started each stopped service without hesitation, indicating a clear understanding of the task.
